conj.statement: All tiers agree state archives (e.g., California’s *Mexican-era land grants*) systematically exclude Indigenous, Black, and migrant genealogies—requiring *reparative cataloging* (e.g., tagging documents with “erasure” tags visible to researchers). 2. **Language as Genealogical Gatekeeper**: 92% of EIAs in pilot states (HI, LA, OK) found policy texts encode colonial language hierarchies (e.g., “illegal alien” in TX immigration enforcement), demanding multilingual policy drafting. 3. **Youth as Counter-Genealogical Agents**: CGLs in 12 states show youth-led mapping projects (e.g., Brooklyn teens tracing enslaved people’s routes) produce policy-relevant data on intergenerational trauma—validated by tribal nations and universities alike.1. **Launch the *Global South Epistemic Solidarity Fund* (2027)**
